chunks.text: The opendetail package exposes a small CLI: build (index your docs), setup (scaffold config and route), and doctor (verify the setup). Run it via npx, bunx, or pnpm dlx so the version matches your package.json.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: Where to start
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
Commands (at a glance)

| Command | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| opendetail build | Write .opendetail/index.json from your config and content globs. |
| opendetail setup | Create opendetail.toml, optional route, optional first build. |
| opendetail doctor | Check files and env for the selected integration mode. |
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text:
One-liner usage

`opendetail build [--config <path>] [--output <path>] [--cwd <path>]
opendetail setup [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--base-path <url>] [--include <glob>] [--media-include <glob>] [--with-media] [--skip-build] [--force] [--interactive] [--no-interactive] [--cwd <path>]
opendetail doctor [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--cwd <path>]
`

For full flags, examples, and copy-paste blocks, open the page for each command above.
chunks.title: CLI

chunks.text: opendetail build is the index build step: it resolves paths from your config, walks the include globs, and writes .opendetail/index.json (or --output).
chunks.title: opendetail build

chunks.text:
Usage

`opendetail build [--config <path>] [--output <path>] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: opendetail build

chunks.text:
Flags

| Flag | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| --config | Path to opendetail.toml (default: ./opendetail.toml). |
| --output | Where to write the index JSON (default: .opendetail/index.json relative to cwd). |
| --cwd | Working directory for resolving paths. |
chunks.title: opendetail build

chunks.text: Examples
chunks.title: opendetail build

chunks.text:
Related

setup can run an initial build after scaffolding.doctor checks that the index file exists and looks sane for your integration mode.
chunks.title: opendetail build

chunks.text:
Each command is a separate page with flags, behavior, and copy-paste examples.

| Command | Role |
| --- | --- |
| build | Read opendetail.toml, match content, write .opendetail/index.json. |
| setup | Scaffold config, optional Next.js route, and optionally run an initial build. |
| doctor | Verify config, built index, route (self-hosted), and required environment variables. |
chunks.title: CLI commands

chunks.text:
Suggested order

setup once per repo (or when you change integration mode).build whenever docs or media globs change.doctor when something fails or after upgrading packages.
chunks.title: CLI commands

chunks.text:
See also

CLI quickstart — install check and first steps.CLI overview — how the binary fits the rest of OpenDetail.
chunks.title: CLI commands

chunks.text: opendetail doctor runs a checklist and prints ✓ or ✗ for each item. It exits with an error if anything required is missing.
chunks.title: opendetail doctor

chunks.text:
Usage

`opendetail doctor [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: opendetail doctor

chunks.text:
Self-hosted expectations

Config: opendetail.toml (or --config) exists.Index: .opendetail/index.json (or configured output) exists.Route: the API route file exists at the configured path.Env: OPENAI_API_KEY is set where the route runs.
chunks.title: opendetail doctor

chunks.text:
Hosted expectations

Config and index as above.Env: OPENDETAIL_ENDPOINT points at your hosted OpenDetail HTTP API.
chunks.title: opendetail doctor

chunks.text: Examples
chunks.title: opendetail doctor

chunks.text:
Related

setup if files are missing.build if the index is stale or absent.
chunks.title: opendetail doctor

chunks.text: The CLI ships in the opendetail package. Prefer npx, bunx, or pnpm dlx so you always run the version pinned by your project.
chunks.title: CLI quickstart

chunks.text:
Check the binary

If that prints a version, the executable resolves correctly from your package manager.
chunks.title: CLI quickstart

chunks.text:
See usage

Running the CLI without a subcommand prints supported commands and example invocations (same as opendetail build, opendetail setup, and opendetail doctor).
chunks.title: CLI quickstart

chunks.text:
Typical flows

Greenfield app: opendetail setup to write opendetail.toml, scaffold the default route (self-hosted), and optionally run an initial index build.Docs changed: opendetail build to refresh .opendetail/index.json.Something looks wrong: opendetail doctor to verify config, index, route, and environment expectations.
chunks.title: CLI quickstart

chunks.text: Next
chunks.title: CLI quickstart

chunks.text: opendetail setup writes the standard project shape for either self-hosted (local Next route + OpenAI) or hosted (client talks to your deployed endpoint).
chunks.title: opendetail setup

chunks.text:
Usage

`opendetail setup [--integration <self-hosted|hosted>] [--config <path>] [--route <path>] [--base-path <url>] [--include <glob>] [--media-include <glob>] [--with-media] [--skip-build] [--force] [--interactive] [--no-interactive] [--cwd <path>]
`
chunks.title: opendetail setup

chunks.text:
What it can write

opendetail.tomlIn self-hosted mode, a route at src/app/api/opendetail/route.ts by default (override with --route).opendetail/index.json unless you pass --skip-build
chunks.title: opendetail setup

chunks.text:
Important flags

| Flag | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| --integration | self-hosted or hosted. |
| --config | Custom config path. |
| --route | Custom route file path (self-hosted). |
| --base-path | Public source URL base (default /docs). |
| --include | Docs glob (default matches typical content/**/*.{md,mdx}). |
| --media-include | Media glob when media is enabled. |
| --with-media | Include [media] in config. |
| --skip-build | Skip the initial index build. |
| --force | Overwrite generated files. |
| --interactive / --no-interactive | Control the setup wizard when stdin is a TTY. |
| --cwd | Run against another working directory. |
chunks.title: opendetail setup

chunks.text: Examples
chunks.title: opendetail setup

chunks.text:
Related

build for rebuilding the index after docs change.doctor to verify the result.
chunks.title: opendetail setup

chunks.text: OpenDetail reads opendetail.toml at the project root.
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Minimal config

`version = 1
include = ["content/**/*.{md,mdx}"]
exclude = []
base_path = "/docs"
`
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Fields

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| version | yes | Must be 1 |
| include | yes | Glob patterns for Markdown and MDX source files |
| exclude | no | Ignore globs for files matched by include |
| base_path | no | URL base used when source URLs are generated |
| media | no | Rules for mapping local media references to public URLs |
| remote_resources | no | OpenAI Responses API retrieval tools at answer time |
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Base path

base_path is normalized before URLs are generated. Match it to the public route where your docs are served, for example /docs or /help.
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Media

Use [media] when docs reference local images such as ./hero.png.

`[media]
include = ["content/**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,webp,avif,gif,svg}"]
exclude = []
base_path = "/content-media"
`

When media is configured:

http:// and https:// URLs are returned as-isroot-relative /... URLs are returned as-islocal relative paths are mapped only if they match [media].includeunsupported absolute schemes and protocol-relative URLs are ignored

If a doc references a local image that does not match the configured media globs, the build fails with a clear error.
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text:
Remote resources

Optional Responses API tools:

`[remote_resources.file_search]
vector_store_ids = ["vs_123"]
max_num_results = 8

[remote_resources.web_search]
allowed_domains = ["platform.openai.com", "docs.example.com"]
search_context_size = "low"
`

file_search

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| vector_store_ids | yes | OpenAI vector store IDs |
| max_num_results | no | Limit from 1 to 50 |

web_search

| Field | Required | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| allowed_domains | no | Restrict search to listed domains |
| search_context_size | no | low, medium, or high |

Remote citations appear in sources with kind: "remote".
chunks.title: Configuration

chunks.text: opendetail-fumadocs is optional glue when your docs site already uses Fumadocs (or the same URL patterns): it turns assistant source items into safe href values for the UI — local pages your app knows about, or external HTTPS links.
chunks.title: opendetail-fumadocs

chunks.text:
API

resolveFumadocsSourceTarget — Given knownPageUrls and a source (kind: "local" or "remote"), returns { external, href } or null for unknown local paths.createFumadocsSourceTargetResolver(knownPageUrls) — Returns a ResolveAssistantSourceTarget you pass to opendetail-react assistant components (e.g. AssistantSidebarShell resolveSourceTarget).getFumadocsPageUrls(source) — Maps a Fumadocs-style source with getPages() to a flat list of URLs for the allowlist.

Local URLs must match a known pathname (no hash required for the allowlist match; hashes are preserved on output). Unknown paths like /docs/old-page resolve to null so the UI does not invent links.
chunks.title: opendetail-fumadocs
opendetail-fumadocs

chunks.text:
OpenDetail turns the Markdown and MDX you already ship into grounded answers inside your product: same content your team edits, retrieved with citations instead of vibes. It is intentionally small: a build step, a local index file, a narrow HTTP contract, and thin adapters — not a second CMS and not a generic chat platform.

From your app root, the CLI can scaffold config, an optional Next.js route, and your first index build:
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text:
How the pieces connect

Your docs and media live in the repo. Build turns matched files into .opendetail/index.json. When someone asks a question, your route or client loads that index, picks the best chunks with lexical search, and calls the OpenAI Responses API using only that context — plus any remote tools you configured. The UI receives streamed NDJSON (text, sources, images) so you can render like a live dev tool instead of blocking on a full response.

The same pages stay readable as normal docs. Machine-readable routes (for example /llms.mdx/docs) stay available for tooling without maintaining a parallel format.
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text:
Who it is for

Product and devrel teams who want in-app help without training a separate knowledge base.Developers who want a boring, testable pipeline: build artifact in git, predictable API, clear errors.Agents that need stable URLs and structured retrieval — the index and routes are built for that use case too.
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text:
Packages (at a glance)

| Package | Role |
| --- | --- |
| opendetail | Build, core runtime, CLI (opendetail binary). |
| opendetail-next | Next.js route helpers for self-hosted mode. |
| opendetail-react | useOpenDetail and streaming client for browser UI. |
| opendetail-fumadocs | Optional helpers when you already use Fumadocs-style docs. |
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text:
Where to go next

Core: Overview · Configuration · CLI · Runtime

Adapters: Next.js · React · Fumadocs
chunks.title: Documentation

chunks.text:
Self-hosted (default, full path today)

Self-hosted means: your Next.js route, your Node runtime, your API key, your rebuilt index on deploy.

The App Router integration lives in opendetail-next: createNextRoute, createNextRouteHandler, and renderNextSourceLink for MDX. See opendetail-next for exports, options, and requirements.

Requirements (summary)

Node.js 18+Route handler on the Node.js runtime (not Edge) for the default adapterOPENAI_API_KEY set.opendetail/index.json present after build

Request body — intentionally tiny:

`{ "question": "How do I configure base_path?" }`

Malformed bodies get a JSON error instead of a broken stream.

Response — application/x-ndjson; charset=utf-8, events in this order:

meta — model metadatasources — matched chunks (and remote citations when configured)images — grounded image references from matched docsdelta — streamed answer textdone — final texterror — safe public error payload

If initialization fails before streaming starts, you get JSON error instead of NDJSON. In production, repeated init failures are cached so you do not pay the same failure cost on every request.
chunks.title: Integration

chunks.text:
Hosted-shaped (scaffold only for now)

Hosted mode in the CLI does not generate a route. It still writes config and builds .opendetail/index.json, but your app is expected to call a remote endpoint you configure — the OpenDetail-managed cloud backend is not shipped yet.

Point the client at your future endpoint with environment variables:

OPENDETAIL_ENDPOINT (server)OPENDETAIL_AUTH_TOKEN (optional)In the browser, examples often use NEXT_PUBLIC_OPENDETAIL_*

Hook shape:

Request and stream events stay the same as self-hosted so you do not need a breaking migration later.

Rebuild and diagnose commands are in Quickstart and CLI.
chunks.title: Integration

chunks.text:
How grounding works (short version)

Build time — Matched files are parsed, split into heading-based chunks, and written to .opendetail/index.json. No required vector database in the default path.Request time — The runtime loads the index, runs lexical search, sends the best chunks to the Responses API, and optionally attaches remote tools from config.Instructions — The model is told to answer from those sources, cite factual statements, avoid inventing coverage, and admit when the topic is not in the docs.

If nothing matches well, you still get a response, but it may be marked as fallback; if the model produces no usable text, users see a clear “not documented yet” style message (see Runtime).
chunks.title: Integration

images.title: Thread shell — composer anchored, reading column
Streaming response — sources and media alongside partial text

chunks.text:
UI references (this site)

These SVGs show patterns you might mirror: a narrow reading column with a fixed composer, and streaming layout with sources and images.

Thread shell reference

Streaming response reference
chunks.title: Integration

chunks.text:
Quick FAQ (inline)

Chat UI? OpenDetail is the engine and contracts; you bring the UI (or use examples from the registry).Vector DB? Not required for the default local index path.MDX with components? Text and headings matter for retrieval; exotic runtime components are not the retrieval unit.Hosted backend? Scaffolded client shape exists; managed backend is not shipped in this phase.
chunks.title: Integration

chunks.text: opendetail-next wraps the core runtime for self-hosted deployments on the Next.js App Router: a POST handler that validates the request body, streams NDJSON, and optional renderNextSourceLink for assistant citations in MDX.
chunks.title: opendetail-next
opendetail-next

chunks.text:
Request and response

The handler accepts POST with JSON { "question": string }. Malformed bodies return a JSON error.

Successful responses use application/x-ndjson; charset=utf-8. Event order and semantics match Integration (meta, sources, images, delta, done, error). Initialization failures before streaming return JSON instead of NDJSON.
chunks.title: opendetail-next

chunks.text:
Source links in MDX

For Fumadocs-style docs, pass renderNextSourceLink from opendetail-next into your assistant UI so local /docs/... citations use Next Link and external URLs stay safe:

`import { renderNextSourceLink } from "opendetail-next";
`

Use with resolveSourceTarget from opendetail-fumadocs when you need known-page URL allowlists.
chunks.title: opendetail-next

chunks.text: The opendetail package is the engine: it reads opendetail.toml, builds .opendetail/index.json, and exposes createOpenDetail for answering questions from that index. The opendetail CLI is the same codebase (build, setup, doctor).
chunks.title: opendetail (core)

chunks.text:
What ships in the package

| Area | Role |
| --- | --- |
| Build | Parse matched Markdown/MDX, chunk by headings, write the index artifact (used by the CLI and programmatic buildOpenDetailIndex). |
| Runtime | Load the index, lexical search, call the Responses API, stream NDJSON — createOpenDetail. |
| CLI | Project setup, index builds, doctor checks — CLI. |
| Config | opendetail.toml schema and validation — Configuration. |
chunks.title: opendetail (core)

chunks.text:
When to use it directly

Use opendetail without Next or React when you integrate from Node (custom server, scripts, tests). For a Next.js App Router route, use opendetail-next; for browser UI, use opendetail-react.
chunks.title: opendetail (core)

chunks.text: Follow this page in order once; after that, rebuild whenever docs change:
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Install

Add the packages you need for a Next.js app with the default route adapter:

Use the copy control to cycle package managers (npm i, Yarn, pnpm, or Bun) with the same dependency list. Check that the CLI is available without a global install:
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Scaffold

Generate opendetail.toml, the default API route, and the first index (unless you skip the build):

What that can do:

write opendetail.tomlscaffold src/app/api/opendetail/route.ts (self-hosted default)run an initial build to produce .opendetail/index.json (use the build command above)

Add OPENAI_API_KEY to the environment your route runs in before you call the API.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Default config shape

You should see something like:

`version = 1
include = ["content/**/*.{md,mdx}"]
exclude = []
base_path = "/docs"

[media]
include = ["content/**/*.{png,jpg,jpeg,webp,avif,gif,svg}"]
exclude = []
base_path = "/content-media"
`

Tweak include / base_path to match where your docs actually live. Full field list is in Configuration.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Rebuild when content changes

Any time Markdown, MDX, or matched media changes:

Run that in CI before deploy, or as a postbuild step, so production always matches the docs you shipped.

Diagnose when something looks wrong:
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text:
Ask the first question

With the default route, POST JSON:

`{ "question": "How do I install this?" }`

Use createOpenDetailClient or useOpenDetail from opendetail-react if you are building UI — see React for options like persistence and headers.

How the route behaves in production, hosted-shaped setups, and grounding are covered in Integration.
chunks.title: Quickstart

chunks.text: Package: opendetail-react. For a Next.js route that serves the same NDJSON stream, see opendetail-next.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
React hook

opendetail-react exports useOpenDetail for browser state and streaming UI.
chunks.title: React
React

chunks.text:
Options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| endpoint | Shortcut for transport.endpoint |
| transport.endpoint | Request URL, defaults to /api/opendetail |
| transport.headers | Static headers or a function returning headers |
| transport.credentials | Fetch credentials mode |
| transport.fetch | Custom fetch implementation |
| persistence.key | Storage key for thread state |
| persistence.storage | local or session, default local |
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Returned state

messages, question, setQuestion, submit, stop, clearThread, status, and error fields such as error, errorCode, errorRetryable, errorStatus.

Assistant messages track streamed text, sources, images, model, duration label, error metadata, and status: pending | streaming | complete | error.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Persistence

When enabled, thread and input are restored; interrupted streams are marked honestly after refresh.
chunks.title: React
React

chunks.text:
Client options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| endpoint | Request URL, default /api/opendetail |
| headers | Static or function |
| credentials | Fetch credentials mode |
| fetch | Custom fetch implementation |
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Request

`{ "question": "..." }`

Headers: accept: application/x-ndjson, application/json, content-type: application/json.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Status values

idle → pending → streaming → idle or error.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Stream event types

meta, sources, images, delta, done, error.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text:
Stop behavior

stop() aborts the active request. Calling submit() while a request is active stops the previous one first.
chunks.title: React

chunks.text: Use opendetail core when calling from Node without Next.js helpers.
chunks.title: Runtime
Runtime

chunks.text:
Assistant instructions

Loaded in order:

assistantInstructions optionassistantInstructionsPath option.opendetail/OPENDETAIL.mdOPENDETAIL.md at project root

If none exist, only built-in grounding instructions apply.
chunks.title: Runtime

chunks.text:
Remote retrieval

Configure via remoteResources in code or remote_resources in opendetail.toml (file_search, web_search).
chunks.title: Runtime

chunks.text:
Defaults

| Setting | Default |
| --- | --- |
| model | gpt-5.4-mini |
| reasoningEffort | none |
| verbosity | low |
| store | false |
| promptCacheRetention | in-memory |
chunks.title: Runtime

chunks.text:
Important options

| Option | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| cwd | Working directory for config and instruction files |
| indexPath | Custom path to the built index |
| indexData | Preloaded index artifact |
| client | Reuse an existing OpenAI client |
| assistantInstructions | Inline project guidance |
| assistantInstructionsPath | Custom instructions path |
| promptCacheKey | Explicit cache key override |
| remoteResources | Override remote retrieval config |
chunks.title: Runtime

chunks.text:
Fallback behavior

If no local chunks match well, OpenDetail may still respond but sets fallback: true. If the model returns no usable text:

`This isn't documented in the configured docs yet.`
chunks.title: Runtime
